summary: Well-structured script and workflow. aggregate.sh uses set -euo pipefail, quoted variables, proper die() error handling, and good function decomposition. XML parsing via regex is justified (avoids xmllint dependency) and handles common JUnit shapes. Workflow has sensible triggers (push/PR/schedule), minimal permissions, and clear comments about nullglob/act behavior. Minor issues: (1) actions/checkout@v4 should pin to full SHA for supply-chain security; (2) SC2016 disable comment is misleading—backticks in printf string are literals, not subshells, and disable is unnecessary; (3) comment claims exit code 2 for parse errors but all errors use die() with code 1. These don't affect correctness but reduce polish. The workflow design assumes fixture files exist elsewhere (upstream matrix jobs)—reasonable for production but worth documenting. jq dependency is checked appropriately at parse_json level.
